Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: The problem requires constructing a set of bitstrings of a fixed length that extends a given set of initial bitstrings. The primary constraint is a prefix-free requirement: no bitstring in the final set can be a prefix of another bitstring in the set. This is equivalent to ensuring that the set of strings forms a prefix code. The interaction between input elements arises from the existing prefixes; if one input string is a prefix of another, the construction must ensure that the extensions do not violate the prefix-free property for any pair of strings. The decision-making process involves determining the optimal way to append bits to each existing string to reach the target length. A choice made for one string (the bits appended) limits the available bit-space for other strings that share a common prefix. The state-space can be modeled as a traversal of a binary tree where the input strings represent paths to certain nodes. The goal is to extend these paths to leaves at a specific depth without any path being a subset of another. The specific invariants to maintain are the prefix-free property across the entire set and the requirement that every string reaches the exact target length. The optimization aspect involves managing the available branching factor in the binary tree to accommodate all required extensions without running out of unique paths.
